title: Quorum Queues, Raft Replication, Availability, and Data Safety · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first quorum queue safety and availability decision record for quorum queues, raft replication, availability, and data safety. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of quorum queue safety and availability decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.
Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.
Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Quorum Use Case Fit
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 3064 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Quorum Use Case Fit.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Majority Loss Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop messaging team handles case 2298 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Majority Loss Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Automatic reconnect immediately restores a quorum queue that permanently lost its majority.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Majority Loss Recovery condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Majority Loss Recovery
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Majority Loss Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop messaging team handles case 3917 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Majority Loss Recovery.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Majority Loss Recovery, keep Majority Loss Recovery connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.
